channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Yohanna Kusau: Champion of Primary Healthcare in Nigeria
item.description: Yohanna Kusau, a primary healthcare worker in Nigeria, has become a symbol of dedication and leadership in the fight to improve community health. His story highlights how small, consistent actions can...

item.title: Gen Z Movements and Youth-led Protests in South Asia: A Comparative Analysis
item.description: The article explores the four key factors driving youth-led protests that ousted leaders in Sri Lanka, Bangladesh, and Nepal, while questioning India's unique position. Educated unemployment at scale ...

item.title: FIFA Considers Hosting World Cup in the US Again by 2038
item.description: The article discusses FIFA's growing interest in hosting the FIFA World Cup in the United States as early as 2038, driven by significant revenue from ticket sales and hospitality. Donald Trump has pub...

item.title: Sharon Stone's Journey to Drug-Free Living After Stroke
item.description: Hollywood icon Sharon Stone has shared her challenging decision to discontinue her stroke medication and cannabis use, aiming for a completely substance-free lifestyle. Despite strict medical warnings...

item.title: U.S. Announces New Tariffs to Replace Expiring Global Levy
item.description: The United States is preparing to implement new tariffs targeting over 60 trading partners, replacing the temporary 10% global levy imposed by President Donald Trump. These new tariffs, ranging betwee...

item.title: Govender brothers face murder trial after witness alleges prior threats
item.description: The trial of Ferrel and Darren Govender, accused of murdering Shailen Singh in December 2024, commenced at the Durban High Court. A confidential witness testified that Ferrel Govender threatened him i...

item.title: Trump Announces Plan to Impose Tariffs on Generic Drugs by 2028
item.description: President Donald Trump announced during a press briefing that the U.S. plans to impose tariffs on generic drugs by 2028, citing the need to protect domestic pharmaceutical companies and reduce relianc...

item.title: Celebrity divorces and personal struggles dominate headlines
item.description: The article compiles a series of celebrity news stories highlighting dramatic life events. It begins with Davina McCall's remarriage to hairdresser Michael Douglas, showcasing their lavish Kent weddin...

item.title: Legal Expert Warns of Trump's Election Lawyers' Plans
item.description: Legal expert Bennito L. Kelty warns about the potential risks posed by Trump's election lawyers, including Kurt Olsen and Cleta Mitchell, who are part of the administration's legal team. Kelty, a jour...

item.title: Nollan Wells' death sparks community mourning and ongoing investigation
item.description: Hundreds gathered at a Mississippi church to mourn 18-year-old Nolan Wells, whose body was found in water near a barrier island two weeks after his disappearance. The funeral service, attended by fami...

item.title: Federal University Wukari Stops All Sign-Out Celebrations on Campus Over Safety and Security Concerns
item.description: The Senate of the Federal University Wukari (FUW) in Taraba State has approved a complete ban on all sign-out and graduation celebration activities within the university campus and nearby areas. The d...

item.title: Collaborative Initiative Enhances Climate Resilience in Kigoma Through Education
item.description: A climate resilience initiative focused on weather information literacy has been launched in Tanzania’s Kigoma Region, targeting 30,000 residents in areas vulnerable to environmental degradation. The ...

item.title: Access Blocked: Reader Unable to View News Article Due to Bot Detection
item.description: The article details an error message encountered by users attempting to access news content on News Corp Australia's website. The message indicates that the user has been detected and blocked as a cra...

item.title: India lodges protest with Russia after missile strike on commercial vessel kills four Indian seafarers
item.description: India has formally summoned Russia's Charge d'Affaires, Vladimir Ladanov, to register a strong diplomatic protest following a missile strike on the commercial vessel MV Golden Leo that resulted in the...

item.title: Calgary Community and Police Search for Neurodivergent 11-Year-Old Boy
item.description: An 11-year-old neurodivergent and non-verbal boy, Parker, went missing from his day home in northwest Calgary last Thursday. Despite a massive search effort involving over 400 police officers and hund...

item.title: Enugu Air Showcases Aviation Vision at Global Airshow
item.description: Enugu Air is making waves in the global aviation scene by participating in the 2026 Farnborough International Airshow, a major event in the UK that brings together over 1,600 exhibitors. The airline’s...

item.title: Boy Harsher's New Album ‘Get Mean’ Explores Themes of Heartbreak and Survival
item.description: Boy Harsher, the electronic duo, have announced their new album ‘Get Mean’, set for release on September 18 via Atlantic Records. The project, which follows their 2022 soundtrack for ‘The Runner’, del...

item.title: Queensland Budget Estimates Hearing Examines Dutton's QIC Board Appointment

item.title: Hantavirus outbreak claims three lives in Venezuela
item.description: A recent report from Agence France-Presse highlights a deadly hantavirus outbreak in Venezuela, where the disease has claimed three lives. The article emphasizes the severity of the situation, noting ...

item.title: Calgary Council Approves Rezoning Exemptions for Specific Properties
item.description: Calgary city council is set to vote on which properties will retain blanket rezoning exemptions, a decision that could impact property owners and urban development plans. The proposed resolution aims ...
